title: IPv6 SSRF guard incomplete: only loopback/unspecified checked, misses fc00::/7 ULA
description: server.rs:636: IpAddr::V6(v6) => v6.is_loopback() || v6.is_unspecified() — this guards ::1 (loopback) and :: (unspecified) but misses IPv6 unique-local addresses (fc00::/7, e.g. fd00::1), IPv6 link-local (fe80::/10), and the IPv4-mapped range (::ffff:10.0.0.1 which maps to private IPv4). An attacker can supply a follower actor URL with host 'fd00::1' and bypass the SSRF check entirely. Rust 1.80's IpAddr::V6 has is_unique_local() (nightly) but not stable; use a manual prefix check: (v6.segments()[0] & 0xfe00) == 0xfc00 for ULA, v6.segments()[0] == 0xfe80 for link-local. Also check IpAddr::V6::to_ipv4_mapped().
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
